---
title: "Introduction & Methods"
---
:::{.callout-warning}
Under development
:::


| Date | Topic | Reading | Resources |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Thurs 9/27 | [Syllabus](index.qmd), [assignments](requirements.qmd), [schedule](schedule.md) | None | [<span class="purple-badge">Poll ↗</span>](https://www.menti.com/bl6rvxw8d8o1) | 
| Tues 9/1 | Computation, algorithm, and implementation<br>The classical model| SGN Ch. 1, *Introduction* (p. 1-17) | [<span class="blue-badge">Slides ↗</span>](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1S-twq_R9KZWHKwUxCgpskvh7g66OaMdo1Z-OYOKt820/edit?slide=id.g3d423b79775_0_0#slide=id.g3d423b79775_0_0)|
| Thurs 9/3 |  Brain geography | SGN Ch. 2, *The Toolbox* (p. 18-25) | [UBC: The Cortex](https://www.neuroanatomy.ca/modules/Cortex/story.html)
| Tues 9/8 | *No class (Monday schedule)* | | 
| Thurs 9/10 | Research methods | SGN Ch. 2, *The Toolbox* (p. 25-43)   | [<span class="blue-badge">Slides ↗</span>](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p) 
| Tues 9/15 | Reading scientific papers| McCabe & Castel 2008 | | 
| Thurs 9/17 | <span class="exam-badge">Exam #1</span>



::: {.callout-tip}
# Learning Goals: Unit 1
**Course**

1. Know who your professor is  
2. Know how I will assess your mastery of course content

**Introduction**

1. Recognize that there are multiple levels at which one can understand a cognitive system (computational, algorithmic, implementation)  
2. Understand the argument for studying a system at all three of these levels 
3. Summarize the classical model of language in the brain
4. Define the terms *localization* and *lateralization*
5. Explain the difference between *functional specialization* and *functional specificity*

**Brain Geography**

1. Tell which way a brain is "facing" based on its orientation
2. Identify and distinguish *coronal*, *axial*, and *sagittal* views of the human brain
3. "Navigate" to a region of the brain using the following neuroanatomical and directional terms: *frontal*, *temporal*, *parietal*, *occipital*, *anterior*, *posterior*, *inferior*, *superior*, *lateral*, *medial*, *sulcus*, *gyrus*

**The Toolbox**

1. Understand the difference between spatial and temporal resolution
2. Know the names of the major research tools that are used to study language in the brain, what is measured by each tool, and the major strengths and weaknesses of each method
3. Understand how scientific papers are meant to be read
:::
